gt_report: There are bilateral pleural effusions which have increased significantly in size when compared to the prior study.  There is associated atelectasis of both lower lobes.  The aerated pulmonary parenchyma is unremarkable with no suspicious lesions identified.  The large airways are patent without evidence of an obstructive lesion.  There is global cardiomegaly with significant coronary artery and mitral anulus calcification.  The pulmonary arteries are enlarged suggestive of pulmonary hypertension.  There is significant calcification of the thoracic aorta.  No aneurysm.  No pathologically enlarged axillary, hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.    There is pneumobilia within the liver, this was evident on the prior study.  There is moderate sized hiatus hernia.  The upper abdominal viscera are otherwise unremarkable on this non dedicated study.  There is a significant kyphoscoliosis, no destructive bone lesion.
prediction: The visualized portion of the thyroid appears unremarkable.  There is no axillary, hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.  The aorta is normal in caliber as is the pulmonary arterial trunk.  There is no pericardial or pleural effusion.  The tracheobronchial tree is patent to the subsegmental level.  The lungs demonstrate a reticular pattern densities along with areas of ground-glass opacity and bronchiectasis that affects the bases more than the apices.  Subpleural bullae are present, more prominent in the apices.  The previously described area of right apical consolidation has near-completely resolved.  No appreciable air trapping is noted.  The extent of disease is similar to prior exams.  Post-right lower lobe wedge resection changes are noted.  The visualized portion of the upper abdomen is unremarkable.  The bones demonstrate degenerative changes of thoracic spine but no aggressive appearing lytic or sclerotic lesions.

gt_report: As compared to the previous examination, ground-glass density peribronchial nodules have newly occurred in the right and left upper lobe (4, 17), in the middle lobe (4, 37), in the right lower lobe (4, 47), and in the right lower lobe (4, 50).  The distribution and morphology of the lesion suggests an infectious cause.  There is a large hiatal hernia.  Extensive coronary calcification.  Unchanged aspect of pre-existing subpleural bilateral bullae.  The pre-existing areas of parenchymal scarring are unchanged.  At the site of surgery, notably along the staple line, there is no evidence for local recurrence.  In the imaged parts of the abdomen, there is no evidence of adrenal lesions.  Status post cholecystectomy.
